region_of_origin: China / Singapore (Chinese cuisine adapted for Singapore dining)
food_subgroup: Vegetable-based mixed dish with mushrooms and stir-fried seasonal vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for grilled mushrooms with stir-fried vegetables. FOODON_03311737 (stir-fried vegetable dish) is the closest parent category covering the stir-fry preparation and vegetable-dominant composition.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (mushrooms and other fungi, cooked) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a combined grilled mushroom and stir-fried vegetable dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific mixed vegetable and mushroom dish.

main_ingredients: mushrooms (e.g. shiitake, oyster, or button mushrooms)
mixed seasonal vegetables (e.g. broccoli, bell peppers, snap peas, carrots, baby corn)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of grilled mushrooms with stir-fried vegetables is approximately 250 g, presented as a shared side dish or individual vegetable main at a Chinese restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mushrooms, cooked, stir-fried; Mixed vegetables, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked mushrooms (NDB 11260 / FDC 169251), stir-fried mixed vegetables (FDC 169967), and minor contributions from soy sauce and sesame oil. The dish is approximately 50% mushrooms and 50% mixed vegetables by cooked weight, with small amounts of oil and seasoning. Energy and macros are cross-checked against FAO/INFOODS SEA vegetable dish reference ranges. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of vegetables and mushrooms — analogous estimate based on cooked mixed vegetables and mushrooms
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this specific dish. GI estimated at 15 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked mushrooms (GI ~10–15) and mixed cooked non-starchy vegetables (GI ~15). The dish is predominantly non-starchy vegetables and mushrooms with minimal added carbohydrate from sauces. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (15 × 16.25) / 100 ≈ 2.4, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 6.5 × 2.5 = 16.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: This dish contains approximately 320 mg sodium per 100 g (800 mg per restaurant serving), primarily from soy sauce and oyster sauce; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size to manage daily sodium intake below 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low glycemic load from non-starchy vegetables and mushrooms
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 15 and a glycemic load of ~2.4 per serving, this dish has minimal impact on postprandial blood glucose and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, high dietary fibre, and beneficial phytonutrients from mushrooms and vegetables
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content (0.5 g per 100 g), absence of cholesterol, and high dietary fibre (2.1 g per 100 g) make this dish cardioprotective; however, sodium from sauces should be monitored in individuals with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Mushrooms with Stir-fried Vegetables (烤蘑菇炒时蔬) is a Chinese-style vegetable dish featuring mushrooms that are first grilled or charred to develop a smoky, umami-rich flavour, then combined with a medley of stir-fried seasonal vegetables such as broccoli, bell peppers, snap peas, baby corn, and carrots. The dish is seasoned with garlic, ginger, soy sauce, oyster sauce, and a finishing drizzle of sesame oil, producing a savoury (咸鲜) flavour profile with tender-crisp textures. It is a popular offering at Chinese restaurants in Singapore, often served as a shared side dish or a light vegetable main. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories (~62 kcal per 100 g), low in fat, and provides meaningful dietary fibre (2.1 g per 100 g), potassium (310 mg), and B-vitamins including riboflavin and niacin from the mushrooms. The glycemic index is low (~15), making it suitable for blood glucose management. The primary nutritional concern is sodium from soy and oyster sauces (~320 mg per 100 g), which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian-friendly and halal-compatible when prepared without animal-derived oyster sauce substitutes.
common_user_queries: How many calories in grilled mushrooms with vegetables?
Is 烤蘑菇炒时蔬 good for diabetics?

meal_context: shared side dish at Chinese restaurant
part of a multi-dish Chinese family-style meal
suitable for vegetarian diners at Chinese restaurants in Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact match for this dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked mushrooms and stir-fried mixed vegetables, cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA vegetable dish ranges. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for comparable non-starchy vegetable dishes. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked mushrooms and stir-fried mixed vegetables; no single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish. 2. Sodium content (320 mg per 100 g) reflects typical Chinese restaurant seasoning with soy sauce and oyster sauce; home-prepared versions may have significantly lower sodium. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish containing no animal products; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for mushroom and vegetable components — naturally zero for this plant-based dish. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as no hydrogenated fats are used in this preparation — naturally zero. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as the dish contains no animal-derived ingredients — naturally zero. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value 1.1 estimated from vegetable oil composition). 7. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked non-starchy vegetables and mushrooms; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 8. GL per serving = (15 × 16.25) / 100 ≈ 2.4, based on 250 g serving size. 9. is_vegan set to false because oyster sauce (蚝油) is a standard ingredient in this dish as served at See Ya Tonight restaurant; a vegan version would require substitution. 10. Price SGD 28.60 reflects the field dataing price from field survey data (See Ya Tonight 今晚有局); this is a single-restaurant data point and may not reflect market-wide pricing.
